title: Rivian R2 spotted with LiDAR near HQ, looks better than most LiDARs
summary: Rivian’s new R2 has been spotted near Rivian HQ sporting a LiDAR sensor, which won’t be included with the earliest vehicles but will be added in a few months. And the implementation actually looks pretty good, compared to the large “taxi bumps” present on many other vehicles.

title: Genesis confirms the GV90 launch date as its ultra-luxe SUV nears arrival
summary: The GV90 will go on sale as the largest and most lavish Genesis SUV yet. Genesis announced an official launch date for the GV90 ahead of its upcoming debut.

title: Controversial Ferrari Luce EV is an instant sellout in China [update]
summary:
All the controversy surrounding the launch of the first all-electric sedan to ever wear the Ferrari badge hasn’t slowed down sales any – every single Ferrari Luce allocated to the Chinese market is already spoken for.

UPDATE 29JUN2026: Ferrari dealers say you can still order a Luce in China.

title: $383 million port and freight infrastructure grant to create 22,000 clean jobs
summary: A ceremony held at the Port of Long Beach saw a record-setting $383 million grant from the state’s Port and Freight Infrastructure Program (PFIP) issued to support 22,000 clean jobs, reduce port and freight emissions, and build up a more modern, more sustainable supply chain at one of America’s busiest ports.

title: Big Short’s Michael Burry discloses new Tesla (TSLA) position
summary:
Michael Burry has disclosed a new short position against Tesla (TSLA), revealing that he shorted the stock at $416.22 on Tuesday.

The bet is one piece of a broader basket of new shorts the “Big Short” investor placed against what he describes as an inflating AI and semiconductor bubble.

title: The Chevy Blazer EV avoids a price hike for 2027 despite a few upgrades
summary: The 2027 Chevy Blazer EV will retain the same price as the outgoing model, despite gaining a NACS port and additional features.

title: Solar gets railed: clever Swiss pilot proves PV panels and trains can coexist
summary: Finding places to deploy new solar panels is often just as challenging as the construction project itself – with productive farmland, open parking lots, and rooftops all offering their own sets of pros and cons, but a recent pilot project suggests that thousands of acres of viable, low-cost land have been hiding in plain sight all along: the space between railroad tracks.

title: Tesla poaches 17-year Intel veteran to lead its ‘Terafab’ chip plant
summary:
Tesla has hired a 17-year Intel manufacturing veteran, Gary Jiang, to serve as “Director, Terafab,” the first named leadership hire tied to the automaker’s ambitious Austin chip fab project.

The executive, who started at Tesla this month, most recently ran tool installation and ramp for Intel’s cutting-edge 18A process — exactly the experience Tesla lacks in-house.

title: Electrify America is adding NACS chargers to more of its busiest stations
summary: Electrify America is taking the next step in its shift to the North American Charging Standard (NACS), expanding its pilot program to more of its busiest charging stations in California and along the East Coast.

title: BYD’s flagship electric SUV is so smooth that it won an anti-motion-sickness certification
summary: BYD’s new full-size electric SUV features an industry-first anti-motion-sickness mode for all passengers.

title: Someone Keeps Stealing Entire Truckloads Of Tesla Batteries
summary: Thieves have made off with trailers loaded with Tesla battery packs straight from the factory nearly a dozen times since December.

title: California's New EV Incentive Is Almost Here—With Special Benefits For Rivian And Lucid
summary: One key restriction on the $135 million incentive program won't apply to vehicles from California-based EV brands.

title: BMW iX5 Blows Away The Competition With 460-kW Charging, 435-Mile Range
summary: The iX5 gets the largest battery pack ever installed in an electric BMW.

title: An App Flagged This Tesla Battery As Degraded. The Car Said It Was Worse
summary: A four-year-old Model 3 Performance scored 88% battery health in Tesla's own test—lower than its owner expected, but close to a prediction by a third-party app.

title: Drivers Are Not Happy With Their Cars' Screens
summary: Infotainment screens are the one area in cars where initial quality has gotten worse year-over-year, a study found.

title: Slate's Cheap EV Pickup Is Surprisingly Easy To Make Expensive
summary: Slate's $25,000 electric pickup is refreshingly simple, but its endless menu of accessories can quickly add up to something else.

title: Shell Built A Tiny EV With Hyper-Fast Charging And A New Cooling System
summary: This tiny EV is impressively clever, but the real business case is hiding in the coolant.

title: How Hankook Is Making EV Tires Better
summary: The company's line of iON tires is designed to maximize durability and range. You can experience them yourself at Electrify Expo Demo Days.

title: Tesla Is Giving Away Free Supercharging. Here's How To Win.
summary: Tesla is gamifying charging and will reward the winners with free Supercharging for the life of the car. Here's how to win.

title: The Peaq Is Peak Skoda. Too Bad America Can’t Buy It
summary: The Peaq is a big, sensible, seven-seat electric SUV with serious space and range and a very reasonable price.
